feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business One
feed.subtitle: SAP Business One blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SLD, License and Extension Manager Error Installing SAP Business One 10.0 SP2511 on Win Server 2 SQL
entry.content:
Failed to save database credential to license server
Win32 Error Code 67 - The network name cannot be found
Win32 Error Code 2250 - This network connection does not exist
GetSHRCredentials returned error -304
SLD-config.xml for BAS is not located
Application execution failed with error 25
Application execution failed with error 24
Application execution failed with error -3

entry.title: Updates from SAP Business One Support
entry.content:
2026 is well on it's way, already February. Here are some short updates from the Support Organization.

We have held our first session in our highly successful Webinar Series. The topic this time was Understanding and analyzing Logfiles. The session was delivered and prepared by two absolute experts in the area - Branislav Stark presented the material that he had prepared with Product Area Lead Yvonne Doyle.

The recording and the material can now be found in the KBA – 3525031 - Central KBA for Partner Enablement Sessions delivered by SAP Business One Support. In the KBA you will find the recordings for all 9 sessions that has been delivered to date.

Currently we are preparing for the 10th session in the series - Managing certificates in SAP Business One. The session will go ahead on March 4th, 14.00 CET. Product Area Lead Pavol Hrasko will present assisted by members of his team. 

The registration is now open, the sessions are for partners and they are for free. Follow the link to register - SAP Partner Portal | Managing certificates in SAP Business One. 

Please note, as a customer or a freelance, you can access the KBA and take in the material that way. If you are a Spanish speaker or have people in your team that prefer consuming the sessions in Spanish, we are planning some this spring. Keep an eye out on LinkedIn and the portal. 

Moving on

Edit: As promised, adding updates about FP 2602 - it has been released. Check out What's New, many new features on the Web Client. 

What's New Videos:

What's New 2602 - Banking, Inventory and Logistics, Prod & Planning
What's New FP2602 - CRM, Sales and Purchasing
What's New in SAP Business One 10.0 FP2602: Usability, Analytics & Reports, Platform & Extensibility

The two overview notes 3644596 (HANA) and 3644642 (MSSQL).

Upgrade strategy link. 

That time of year

This is repeat, but we do believe you will benefit from getting this served again. Period End Closing Material. 

There is a lot of different materials that SAP has made available, ranging from basic how-to stuff to more intricate issues that you might run into.

Starting with the basics - How to Perform a Year-End Closing in SAP Business One 10.0 – it takes you through the process of how to prepare and execute the period end in SAP Business One.  

If you are already mastering this topic, then the Guided Answer (GA) for Period End Closing – covers a whole range of areas, a good friend to have when doing the closing. Also, for the experienced user.

Another GA that might come in handy is the one for Financial Statements – again, a useful resource to have available should you not agree with some of the numbers that are presented.

While we like the Guided Answer format and hope you do too, if you prefer to consume the material in the form of an SAP Note, we have a fairly detailed one for you as well - 2940618 - Troubleshooting: Period-End Closing Issues.

The note takes you through issues caused by Settings, User Handling, Database issues and some System Limitations. Much of it, you will also find in the GA previously mentioned.

Last

Short reminder about our Guided Answers. SAP Business One Support has of today released 31 different GAs. You will find all of them linked to the central KBA - 3213852 - Central KBA for SAP Business One Guided Answers

The Support Spotlight Blogs – find them here: Tips from SAP Business One Support

Keep interacting in the communities -  SAP Business One Community

entry.title: Being Bilingual
entry.content:
Dear SAP Business One community

Today I want to talk about how you could manage data in several languages within the web client.

Let’s look at this video to learn more:

 

You can find additional information in the SAP Business One Web client user guide  and the Learning journey.

Best wishes,  Ari

entry.title: Testing UDVs before Copying or Importing
entry.content:
SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602 continues to expand its support for User-Defined Values (UDVs). In this release, additional objects, including UDOs and UDTs are now supported. For the full list of newly supported objects, refer to the online help.

A key enhancement introduced in this version is the ability to test UDVs before copying or importing them. This is especially valuable when you are working with a large number of UDVs and want to ensure their accuracy before committing any changes.

Testing before Copying:

In the central UDV management window, you'll find a new option in the Copy menu: “Copy After Test.”

Choosing this option allows you to run a test on the selected UDVs before they are copied, ensuring everything is valid and ready for use.

Testing before Importing:

When importing UDVs from a file, a new “Test” button appears above the preview table. 

Clicking this button triggers a test of all UDVs in the import file before the actual import begins.

Once the test completes, the results are displayed in the preview table, giving you the confidence to continue with the import process. For more details on the import workflow, see the online help.

With these enhancements, SAP Business One now offers a more streamlined and reliable process for copying and importing UDVs, helping you maintain accuracy and optimize your workflow.

entry.title: Ready, Set, Demo: SAP Business One Releases Updated Localized Demo Databases
entry.content:
We’re happy to announce that the localized demo databases for SAP Business One have been refreshed and upgraded to the newest version, 10.0 FP 2602. These updated databases are now ready for download!

This release includes newly generated transactional data, giving your demos a clean, modern, and polished appearance.

The localized demo databases deliver a complete, pre-configured SAP Business One environment across 39 different localizations, all built on the OEC Computers model company. They are offered for both MS SQL and SAP HANA deployments.

Whether you’re presenting SAP Business One, conducting training, exploring product capabilities, or performing tests, this updated demo environment helps you work with more accuracy, clarity, and real‑world relevance.

Download the latest SAP Business One Localized Demo Databases

Best wishes,  Ari

entry.title: Cash Flow Report in SAP Business One
entry.content:
The Cash Flow report is one of the key reports you can find among the financial reports in SAP Business One. In this article I want to give you some information on how to use this report for your key business solutions

First at all – why is it so important?

The Cash Flow report lets you see and foresee your liquidity balance for a certain period of time with different security levels1 from cash accounts to Business Partners forecast. The following diagram represents the security level of different components that affect your liquidity from the point of view of SAP Business One: 

Let’s see how this information is displayed in the report in SAP Business One2.: 

Like all other reports, the Cash Flow report requires you to enter the time frame to collect data. So, this is the first mandatory step. In this example, I run the report in the middle of the period, so I can see the real data for February and future data for March.

With weekly intervals, I can see the following results:

There are 9 collapsed lines for every 7 days starting from February 1. In the report header you can select or deselect different security levels. The effect of that can be seen in the results. Here you can see the difference once I deselect customer liabilities: 

Customer liabilities are taken away as a liquidity source and as a result there is cash shortage by the end of February. Let’s take a closer look at the customer liabilities now by expanding the relevant week and security level to understand why it is at the end of February and not earlier or later.  

The Invoice 1064 was issued in January with total amount 2,120.00 $. However, in the report it is displayed only in February and only a half of the total amount. This is because amount 1,060.00 $ is taken from the installments table of this Invoice: 

Let’s check how the data changes in case you have some statistics about business partner payment experience and you want to consider that in your report. So, this business partner has this information already in his master data: 

 

And going back to the selection criteria you can add one more criterion: 

 

And see results: the customer liabilities are now moved one week later – 

You can also run the report only for specific business partners in case it is required. For that you can update your selection criteria on the Business Partner tab: 

In this way, SAP Business One will check only the documents that are issued to the business partners you have selected.

Let’s check another security level: cash accounts. First of all, we can select or deselect specific accounts on the selection criteria Cash tab: 

SAP Business One will list here all the accounts for which you have selected Cash Account in their master data:

And secondly, like for the marketing documents SAP Business One operates with the due date of the transaction when running the Cash Flow report, the same happens for the transactions booked to the cash accounts:

Although the payment was made in February, you will be able to use the money only in March. This can be a result of the deferred bank transfers. The due date of the credit card voucher or check is also taken into consideration for the payments with checks or credit cards. In the same way as the customer liabilities, you can deselect this security level in the document header to check what the results will be.

To see how the customer or vendor forecast affects the Cash Flow report, you need to add to your selection criteria anything or all from the following selection criteria: blanket agreements, document drafts, recurring transactions, or marketing documents that are not posted to the business partner account. However, it can affect your cash flow.

Let’s take a look how these documents will affect the report:

Delivery No. 1182 makes changes to the cash balance with date 03/03/26. The date is calculated, according to the payment terms, to be 03/02/26:

Now, after discussing the security levels, let’s continue with the other features of this report.

Let’s start with the first line OB; there’s no value there. The OB is used in SAP Business One as opening balance. And the cash flow allows you to enter the value:

or SAP Business One will calculate that for you:

The Add Closing Balances checkbox is aplicable for the localizations where you close the balance sheets accounts during the year-end transactions. I recommend having it selected there.

Now when I run the report the situation dramatically changes:

You can easily check, by expanding and using navigation arrows, how the data got to the report:

In this case I would recommended to check if some transactions are not booked.

You can also add some transactions that are in your mind, but you do not have any recurring postings for that or journal vouchers. For example, you are going on a business trip, and you plan some business dinners with partners. You can enter the amount to the Include Projected Postings table as below:

And see the results under the Cash Accounts security level (this security level is defined by you in the table above):

In case these expenses or income are regular, you can also define recurrence periods. Any line you enter in this table is saved, so you do not need to enter the same information every time you run the report.

With that I want to finish my story about SAP Business One Cash Flow report, and I wish you good luck in your further discovery of the Cash Flow features. I would recommend checking system Crystal Reports layouts, 😉.

And one more thing: I would like to invite you to provide feedback or ask clarifying questions regarding the topic in the comments section. It is also possible to submit the improvement requests for this feature (and others as well!) on the Customer Influence site: https://influence.sap.com/SAPBusinessOne, and you can also visit the Implementation Arena for useful implementation tips.

For more information, visit SAP Business One training: Generating the Cash Flow, Aging and Dunning Letters Reports

---------------------------------------------------------

  1. A security level is the level of probability that the transaction will turn to cash (incoming and outgoing).
  2. The screenshots are taken from the demo database OEC Computers with the US localization setup.

entry.title: Questions and Answers about E-Invoicing and E-Reporting for France in SAP Business One
entry.content:
On December 29, 2023, the French authorities published legislation in the 2024 budget that included updated timelines for the e-invoicing and e-reporting mandate. According to this legislation, the implementation schedule is as follows:

September 1, 2026: All taxpayers will be required to receive e-invoices. Additionally, the issuance of B2B (business-to-business) e-invoices and e-reporting will become mandatory for large enterprises ("GE") and intermediate-sized enterprises ("ETI").

September 1, 2027: The issuance of B2B e-invoices and e-reporting will become mandatory for small and medium-sized enterprises ("PME") and micro companies ("TPE").

SAP is officially registered as an Agreed Platform (PA) by the French tax authorities, the General Directorate of Public Finance (DGFiP). This registration enables customers to seamlessly comply with France's e-invoicing and e-reporting requirements.

In SAP Business One, the functionality that will support the E-Invoicing and E-Reporting in France is under development. Since partners have been asking many questions in advance, this blog post aims to provide clarity and serve as a helpful resource for all partners and customers who may have similar inquiries.

The summary of the questions and answers can be found here.

Q1:

Will the agreed platform (PA), the mandatory third party in France, be SAP?

Answer

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC), Cloud Edition, will be supported as an approved platform (PA) by SAP Business One. SAP DRC, Cloud Edition, is a platform approved by the French tax authorities. If customers want to use the end-to-end solution, they will have to subscribe to DRC. DRC will be able to receive electronic documents in all three formats (UBL, CII, and Factur-X) and will transform them into UBL format in order to import these electronic documents into SAP Business One.

Q2:

Regarding imports, will the purchase invoice be created in SAP Business One with links to the goods receipt PO?

Answer

Yes, it is possible to link A/P invoice to either a goods receipt PO or a purchase order.

Q3:

Regarding export, will a wizard be used?

Answer

The Document Generation Wizard can be used for this purpose.

Q4:

Will SAP provide a DRC connector for SAP Business One, version for SAP HANA and SAP Business One SQL?

Answer:

It will be delivered for both. It is universal.

Q5:

How do the connections work between DRC SaaS and SAP Business One On-Premise?

Answer:

This part is under development. All details will be provided once completed.

Q6:

SAP has been registered as PA by French local authorities. How will our customers be able to use/subscribe to this service?

Answer:

Please find the link to the SAP help page where the onboarding process is explained in more detail:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/getting-started-with-integration-of-process

Q7:

What components or configurations must we implement? Is there a specific solution or SAP module to which the customer needs to subscribe?

Answer:

After customers complete onboarding for DRC Cloud Edition, they can activate the process: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/activating-processes?locale=en-US 

They need to integrate Peppol Exchange with SAP Business One: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/integrating-peppol-exchange-with-sap-business-one?locale=en-US&q=France+process  and activate processes for France.

How the connection between DRC and SAP  Business One will work is still under development. All details will be announced once development is complete.

Q8:

The SIREN, SIRET, Routing Code, Suffix are required as part of the electronic address. Where can we find them?

Answer:

The SIREN number is available in Business Partner Master Data → Accounting tab → Tax sub-tab.

SIRET number, Routing Code and Suffix are available in Business Partner Master Data → Address tab → Bill To address.

SAP Business One plans to support basic scenarios. The list can be found here:

Additional mappings need to be made by the relevant partner and/or customer to support other industry-specific or use-case-specific scenarios.

Many of open issues might be clarified in this work zone: https://workzone.one.int.sap/site#workzone-home&/groups/CK8AhwChqYjMisy8oYSfgv/workpage_tabs/LxnjcOht6DIhuitgncZLhH 

I hope this Q&A brings more clarity to this topic. Should you have any further questions, please share them in the comment section

entry.title: What’s new in Lifecycle Management for SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602 ?
entry.content:
SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602

 


This release brings powerful Lifecycle Management (LCM) enhancements that streamline how you manage, access, monitor, and secure your SAP Business One environment,
delivering greater efficiency for admins and a smoother experience for end users. 

Key highlights in FP 2602 include:
  • License consumption tracking in License Control Center
  • Certificate expiration visibility directly in System Landscape Directory
  • DATEV export capability in Web Client, powered by Job Service with the new Job Monitor app
  • Support User Session reminders in Web Client
  • Component version visibility in System Landscape Directory
  • New standalone components: Webhook Messenger and Microsoft 365 Integration Service

and more...

Watch the video below to see these updates in action:
▶️ Latest Lifecycle Management (LCM) enhancements in SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602

 

Explore the full details in the What’s New guides:

  • What's New (SAP Business One)
  • What's New (SAP Business One, Version for HANA) 

💡Coming soon:  On my next blog I will walk you through the latest Identity and Access Management (IAM) innovations with Feature Pack 2602,  stay tuned!

entry.title: Latest Identity and Authentication Management features in SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602
entry.content:
Unlock Simpler IAM in SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602
Latest Identity and Authentication Management (IAM) features in SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602 improve how users are authenticated and managed, making IAM implementation simpler and more intuitive than ever.

Key Highlights include:

  • New “Choose Company” modal window
  • Superuser Role assignment during binding and Edit operations
  •  Superuser setting display for bounded company users
  •  Company multi-select during unbinding operation
  • Enhanced UX during binding operation for smoother company selection
  • Support proper display of companies with long name  under “Choose Company” drop down list

▶️ Watch below video for step-by-step demos of these features in action

 

Explore the full details in the What’s New guides:

  • What's New (SAP Business One)
  • What's New (SAP Business One, Version for HANA) 


Elevate security now! implement IAM in SAP B1 10.0 FP 2602 to unlock seamless IdP integration and effortless user binding with minimal setup.

Thank you for reading, catch you on my next blog!

entry.title: Pick List Enhancement : Delivery Creation Based on Pick List in SAP Business One
entry.content:
The evolution of SAP Business One continues to bring powerful enhancements for developers.  With SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2508, a significant new interface was introduced in the DI API to support the creation of target documents from Pick Lists.

This enhancement provides a much-needed, direct method to link documents created from base documents (such as Sales Orders) to Pick List data. This is accomplished using two new properties:

SAPbobsCOM.Documents.Lines.PickLists.PickListEntry

SAPbobsCOM.Documents.Lines.PickLists.PickListLineNum

But how does this new feature perform in the real world? In this blog, we will put it to the test and explore how this functionality behaves under four different inventory configurations and business scenarios. While this blog uses Delivery Notes as examples, the same principles apply to other supported target documents created using the DI API. Let's dive in.

Scenario 1 : Items are NOT managed by Batches / Serial Numbers and Warehouses are NOT allocated by Bin Locations

Case A : Behavior of Delivery creation from one Pick List among multiple Pick Lists for a single Sales Order

  1. Create a Sales Order with a quantity of 10.
  2. Generate three Pick Lists based on the Sales Order with the following quantities:
    • Pick List 1 → Quantity: 2
    • Pick List 2 → Quantity: 3
    • Pick List 3 → Quantity: 5
  3. From Pick List 2, pick a quantity of 3.
  4. Create a Delivery Note based on the Sales Order and Pick List 2 using the DI API code. For example:
SAPbobsCOM.Documents oDocuments = null;
SAPbobsCOM.Document_Lines oDocument_Lines = null;
oDocuments = (SAPbobsCOM.Documents)oCompany.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oDeliveryNotes);
oDocument_Lines = oDocuments.Lines;
oDocuments.CardCode = "C20000";
oDocument_Lines.BaseEntry = 407;
oDocument_Lines.BaseLine = 0;
oDocument_Lines.BaseType = 17;
oDocument_Lines.Quantity = 3;

//Pick List Reference
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListEntry = 57;     //PickList AbsEntry (AbsEntry From PKL1)
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListLineNum = 0;   //PickList Line Number (PickEntry From PKL1)      
int RetVal = oDocuments.Add();

Result:

  • In the DLN1 table, the field PickIdNo is correctly updated with the correct Pick List AbsEntry.
  • Only Pick List 2 (the one used in the Delivery) is closed, while the other two Pick Lists remain open.

Scenario 2 : Items are managed by Batches / Serial Numbers and Warehouses are NOT allocated by Bin Locations

Case A : Behavior of Delivery Creation from a Pick List for a single-line Sales Order with full quantity allocated to a single batch

  1. Create a Sales Order with a quantity of 10, and assign the entire quantity to a single batch (for example: B11).
  2. Generate a Pick List based on the Sales Order and allocate all 10 quantity to the same batch (B11).
  3. Pick the entire quantity (10 quantity) from the Pick List.
  4. Create a Delivery Note based on the Sales Order and Pick List for the full picked quantity using the DI API code. For example:
SAPbobsCOM.Documents oDocuments = null;
SAPbobsCOM.Document_Lines oDocument_Lines = null;
oDocuments = (SAPbobsCOM.Documents)oCompany.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oDeliveryNotes);
oDocument_Lines = oDocuments.Lines;
oDocuments.CardCode = "C20000";
oDocument_Lines.BaseEntry = 408;
oDocument_Lines.BaseLine = 0;
oDocument_Lines.BaseType = 17;
oDocument_Lines.Quantity = 10;

//Batch Allocation
oDocument_Lines.BatchNumbers.BaseLineNumber = 0;
oDocument_Lines.BatchNumbers.BatchNumber = "B11"; 
oDocument_Lines.BatchNumbers.Quantity = 10;

//Pick List Reference
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListEntry = 59;    //PickList AbsEntry (AbsEntry From PKL1)
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListLineNum = 0;  //PickList Line Number (PickEntry From PKL1
int RetVal = oDocuments.Add();​

Result:

  • In the DLN1 table, the field PickIdNo is correctly updated with the correct Pick List AbsEntry.
  • The Pick List is successfully closed after creating the Delivery.

Scenario 3 : Items are NOT managed by Batches / Serial Numbers and Warehouses are allocated by Bin Locations

Case A :  Behavior of Delivery creation from a Pick List for a single-line Sales Order with full quantity allocated to a single bin location

  1. Create a Sales Order with a quantity of 10 in a warehouse that is enabled for bin locations.

  2. Generate a Pick List based on the Sales Order and allocate the full quantity (10 quantity) to a single bin location.

  3. Pick the entire 10 quantity from the specified bin location.

  4. Create a Delivery Note for the picked quantity using the DI API. For example:
SAPbobsCOM.Documents oDocuments = null;
SAPbobsCOM.Document_Lines oDocument_Lines = null;
oDocuments = (SAPbobsCOM.Documents)oCompany.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oDeliveryNotes);
oDocument_Lines = oDocuments.Lines;
oDocuments.CardCode = "C20000";
oDocument_Lines.BaseEntry = 388;
oDocument_Lines.BaseLine = 0;
oDocument_Lines.BaseType = 17;
oDocument_Lines.Quantity = 10;

//Bin Allocation
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.BaseLineNumber = 0;
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.BinAbsEntry = 2;  
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.Quantity = 10;

//Pick List Reference
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListEntry = 36;      //PickList AbsEntry (AbsEntry From PKL1)
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListLineNum = 0;    //PickList Line Number (PickEntry From PKL1)
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListBatchAndBinLineNum = 0;   //PKL2 Line Number (Pkl2LinNum in PKL2 table)
int RetVal = oDocuments.Add();​

Result:

  • In the DLN1 table, the field PickIdNo is correctly updated with the corresponding Pick List AbsEntry.
  • The Pick List is successfully closed after the Delivery is created.

Case B :  Behavior of partial Delivery from a Pick List with quantity allocated across multiple bin locations

  1. Create a Sales Order with a total quantity of 10.
  2. Generate a Pick List based on the Sales Order and allocate quantities across two different bin locations:
    • Line 1 → Quantity: 4
    • Line 2 → Quantity: 6
  3. Perform partial picking as follows:
    • Pick 1 quantity from Line 1
    • Pick 2 quantities from Line 2
  4. Create a Delivery Note for the picked quantity (1 quantity  from Line 1) using the DI API. Example:
SAPbobsCOM.Documents oDocuments = null;
SAPbobsCOM.Document_Lines oDocument_Lines = null;
oDocuments = (SAPbobsCOM.Documents)oCompany.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oDeliveryNotes);
oDocument_Lines = oDocuments.Lines;
oDocuments.CardCode = "C20000";
oDocument_Lines.BaseEntry = 399;
oDocument_Lines.BaseLine = 0;
oDocument_Lines.BaseType = 17;
oDocument_Lines.Quantity = 1;

//Bin Allocation
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.BaseLineNumber = 0;
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.BinAbsEntry = 2;  
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.Quantity = 1; 

//Pick List Reference
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListEntry = 47;     //PickList AbsEntry (AbsEntry From PKL1)
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListLineNum = 0;   //PickList Line Number (PickEntry From PKL1)
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListBatchAndBinLineNum = 0;   //PKL2 Line Number (Pkl2LinNum in PKL2 table)
int RetVal = oDocuments.Add();​

Result:

The Pick List is partially updated:

  • The Line 2 remains unchanged, as it is not referenced in the Delivery document.
  • The SAP Business One client displays the Pick List with updated values, showing the partially delivered quantities.

Scenario 4 : Items are managed by Batches / Serial Numbers and Warehouses are allocated by Bin Locations

Case A : Behavior of partial Delivery from a Pick List with multiple batch and bin location allocations

  1. Create a Sales Order with a total quantity of 10, and allocate the quantity across two different batches.
  2. Generate a Pick List based on the Sales Order.
  3. Assign each batch quantity to different bin locations. For example:
    • Batch B1 → Assigned to one bin location
    • Batch B2 → Assigned to another bin location
  4. The Pick List is displayed in the SAP Business One client reflecting the batch and bin allocations.
  5. Perform partial picking as follows:
    • Pick 1 quantity from the 2nd line
    • Pick 1 quantity from the 3rd line

  6. Create a Delivery Note for the picked quantities (2 quantities in total) using the DI API. Example:
SAPbobsCOM.Documents oDocuments = null;
SAPbobsCOM.Document_Lines oDocument_Lines = null;
oDocuments = (SAPbobsCOM.Documents)oCompany.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oDeliveryNotes);
oDocument_Lines = oDocuments.Lines;
oDocuments.CardCode = "C20000";
oDocument_Lines.BaseEntry = 403;
oDocument_Lines.BaseLine = 0;
oDocument_Lines.BaseType = 17;
oDocument_Lines.Quantity = 2;

//Batch Allocation
oDocument_Lines.BatchNumbers.BaseLineNumber = 0;
oDocument_Lines.BatchNumbers.BatchNumber = "B1"; ;
oDocument_Lines.BatchNumbers.Quantity = 1;
oDocument_Lines.BatchNumbers.Add();
oDocument_Lines.BatchNumbers.BaseLineNumber = 0;
oDocument_Lines.BatchNumbers.BatchNumber = "B2"; ;
oDocument_Lines.BatchNumbers.Quantity = 1;

//Bin Allocation
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.BaseLineNumber = 0;
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.BinAbsEntry = 26;  
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.Quantity = 1;
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.SerialAndBatchNumbersBaseLine = 0;
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.Add();
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.BaseLineNumber = 0;
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.BinAbsEntry = 2;  
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.Quantity = 1;
oDocument_Lines.BinAllocations.SerialAndBatchNumbersBaseLine = 1;

//Pick List Reference
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListEntry = 51;    //PickList AbsEntry (AbsEntry From PKL1)
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListLineNum = 0;  //PickList Line Number (PickEntry From PKL1)
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListBatchAndBinLineNum = 1;   //PKL2 Line Number (Pkl2LinNum in PKL2 table)
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.Add();
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListEntry = 51;   //PickList AbsEntry (AbsEntry From PKL1)
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListLineNum = 0; //PickList Line Number (PickEntry From PKL1)
oDocument_Lines.PickLists.PickListBatchAndBinLineNum = 2;   //PKL2 Line Number (Pkl2LinNum in PKL2 table)
int RetVal = oDocuments.Add();​

Result:

  • The remaining quantities in the Pick List stay open and unchanged.
  • The Pick List is partially updated after the Delivery is created.

Key Takeaways

The introduction of Pick List reference properties in SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2508 provides essential functionality for DI API developers. Here are the key takeaways:

  • Explicit Control: Developers can now programmatically link target documents (e.g., Delivery Notes) to specific Pick Lists using the PickListEntry and PickListLineNum properties.
  • Problem Solved: This resolves the previous system behavior where creating a target document via DI API would close all associated Pick Lists indiscriminately. This fix is reflected in the updates to SAP Notes 3444183 and 2252361.
  • Consistent Application: The logic demonstrated with Delivery Notes is consistently applied across various inventory scenarios and is applicable to all supported target documents created from Pick Lists.

With this enhancement, developers can build more robust and accurate fulfillment automations. For a comprehensive overview of related functionalities, refer to the blog Pick List in SAP Business One SDK.

entry.title: Handling Grid Cell Value Change Events using SAP Business One Web Client UI API Extension
entry.content:
A common requirement observed among partners and customers—whether they are using the classic SAP Business One UI API or the SAP Business One Web Client UI API—is the ability to retrieve event information when the value of a matrix or grid cell changes.

In the classic SAP Business One UI API, this information is documented in the SAP Business One SDK Help, and partners and customers can leverage events such as et_LOST_FOCUS or et_VALIDATE  to handle such scenarios.

However, the challenge arises when using the SAP Business One Web Client UI API Extension: how can event information be retrieved when a grid cell value changes and data validation is required? This blog post explains how to achieve this.

As of SAP Business One version 10.0 SP 2511, you can use the "on" property to add before and after event handlers for controls in a grid cell like the following mentioned format. Please note you do not need to specify the grid's GUID, just specify the Column's GUID (without the COLUMN_ prefix) and the template control inside the column.

"on": [
{
"guid": "xoXg6upQtW5y92TMLUU4te",
"ctrlType": "b1.sdk.Column",
"template": {
"ctrlType": "b1.sdk.Input",
"guid": "CELL_xoXg6upQtW5y92TMLUU4te",
"change": {
"after": "onChangeQuantity"
}
}
},


{
"guid": "wScf8XyBZ9fDKRjV61t5rM_UDF_RDR1_U_L_UDF2",
"ctrlType": "b1.sdk.Column",
"template": {
"ctrlType": "b1.sdk.Input",
"guid": "CELL_wScf8XyBZ9fDKRjV61t5rM_UDF_RDR1_U_L_UDF2",
"change": {
"before": "onChangeUDF"
}
}
}
]

For example, suppose you want to trigger a logic whenever the value in the Unit Price column of a Sales Order row is modified. To capture this event, please follow the steps below:

  • In the layout JSON file, use the “on" overlay and add a “after" event handler to the Unit Price column, indicated by GUID rmzEyqDK55Td8na2cqksVM:
"on": [
{
"guid": "rmzEyqDK55Td8na2cqksVM",
"ctrlType": "b1.sdk.Column",
"template": {
"ctrlType": "b1.sdk.Input",
"guid": "CELL_rmzEyqDK55Td8na2cqksVM",
"change": {
"after": "onChangePrice"
}
}
}
]
  • guid can be retrieved using Web Client Inspector.



  • rmzEyqDK55Td8na2cqksVM is the GUID of the Unit Price column. As mentioned above, it should be specified without the COLUMN_ prefix.
  • CELL_rmzEyqDK55Td8na2cqksVM is the GUID of the Unit Price column cell that can be seen in the image above under the template control.
  • b1.sdk is the global namespace used to store public controls and related types for control properties. This helps organize all controls and their properties in a structured way. For example, to reference the Button control, you would use b1.sdk.Button. To assign a value to the ButtonType property in JavaScript, you would use b1.sdk.ButtonType.Accept.

    In our example, b1.sdk.Column represents an input box type (b1.sdk.Input) and allows users to define properties specific to that column.

  • template is the cell template in the column control. Cell renderer of the column.
  • 'change' is the event name which is fired whenever the value is modified.
  • "onChangePrice" is the function (referencing the controller) that is triggered whenever the value in that column (Unit Price) is changed.

     
  • In the controller, implement the event handler like below:
async onChangePrice(oEnv, oEvent) {
console.log("Price changed");

// Get the updated value, old value and Row Index from the event parameters

const newValue = oEvent.getParameter("value");
const oldValue = oEvent.getParameter("oldValue");
const iRowIndex = oEvent.getParameter("iRowIndex");
console.log("onChangePrice: new value: " + newValue + ", oldValue: " + oldValue.amount + ", iRowIndex: " + iRowIndex);
await oEnv.showMessageBox("Information", `On Row ${iRowIndex} Price changed from ${oldValue.amount} to ${newValue}`,
{
title: models.title,
}
);
}
  • oEnv is the type of the current SDK environment.
  • oEvent is the type of the Web Client event.
  • getParameter gets the event parameters.

Once the event information is triggered, you can proceed with implementing your logic.

We hope this helps you move forward with your implementation.
